title: calophyllum thuriferum
intro: Calophyllum thuriferum, also known as the East Indian sandalwood or yellow sandalwood, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of calophyllolide, a unique compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Calophyllum thuriferum include its use in treating skin conditions, wounds, and fever, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
